name: Pozo de Demo - circular from Verin
name_original: Pozo de Demo – circular desde Verín
region_intro: Jogging routes around Verin offer a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by river valleys, natural parks, and varied terrain. The region features the Támega River, providing ecological corridors, alongside more challenging mountain and hilly areas. These natural features create a range of running experiences, from gentle riverside paths to routes with significant elevation gain. Verin's surroundings provide a mix of surfaces and gradients suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Verin The most popular jogging route is Circular Ruta to Minas dos Carris — Peneda-Gerês National Park, a 13.2 miles (21.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 52 minutes to complete. This route explores the challenging terrain of the Peneda-Gerês National Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Pozo de Demo - circular from Verin, a difficult 9.6 miles (15.5 km) path. This circular route starts directly from Verin, offering accessible running with varied scenery. Local runners also love the Alto de Amoreira via the Mines of the Shadows – Serra do Xurés, a 11.9 miles (19.2 km) trail leading through the Serra do Xurés, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Jogging around Verin is defined by river paths, natural park trails, and hills, offering opportunities for gradient training. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Verin are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 264 runners have used komoot to explore Verin's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running terrain can I expect around Verín?
A: Verín offers a diverse range of running terrain, from gentle riverside paths along the &lt;strong&gt;Támega International Eco-Track&lt;/strong&gt; to more challenging mountain and hilly trails within areas like the &lt;strong&gt;Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll find a mix of surfaces, including softer riverbanks, dirt tracks, and paths with significant elevation changes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate jogging routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed run?
A: Yes, while many routes offer a challenge, there are options for easier runs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Xinzo de Limia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13 km path with minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for a relaxed jog. The region's river beaches and urban green spaces also provide accessible and pleasant areas for leisurely jogs.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Verín area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the routes around Verín are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991629737/pozo-de-demo-circular-from-verin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de Demo - circular from Verín&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 15.5 km loop that starts directly from the town. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24549868" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muiño de Dositeo loop from Allariz-Maceda&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 12.4 km route.

Q: Are there running trails where I can see historical sites or natural landmarks?
A: Yes, several routes combine physical activity with sightseeing. The &lt;strong&gt;Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; features trails like the Geira and Arrieiros Way, which pass by Roman ruins, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3112491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aquis Querquennis Roman Camp&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find routes that lead to picturesque waterfalls, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2542528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo do Demo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or offer views of historic sites like &lt;strong&gt;Monterrei Castle&lt;/strong&gt;.

Q: What are the best seasons for running in Verín?
A: Verín offers good running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at midday, so early morning or late afternoon runs are often preferred. Winter runs are possible, particularly on lower-altitude trails, but it's advisable to check local weather conditions, especially for higher mountain routes.

Q: Are there any running routes that are family-friendly or suitable for strollers?
A: For family-friendly options, consider the flatter sections along the &lt;strong&gt;Támega International Eco-Track&lt;/strong&gt;, which offers a scenic and generally softer surface. While specific stroller-friendly routes are not always explicitly marked, river paths and urban green spaces in Verín often provide accessible, paved, or well-maintained gravel surfaces suitable for families with strollers.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails around Verín?
A: Many natural areas and trails around Verín are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural parks like &lt;strong&gt;Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés&lt;/strong&gt;, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other users. Always carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

Q: Where can I find parking for popular running routes near Verín?
A: Parking availability varies by route. For trails within natural parks like &lt;strong&gt;Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés&lt;/strong&gt;, designated parking areas are usually available at trailheads. For routes starting directly from Verín, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991629737/pozo-de-demo-circular-from-verin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de Demo - circular from Verín&lt;/a&gt;, you can often find public parking within the town or at nearby access points. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information.

Q: Are there any permits required to run in the natural parks near Verín?
A: Generally, permits are not required for casual jogging on established trails within natural parks like &lt;strong&gt;Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; or &lt;strong&gt;Invernadeiro Nature Reserve&lt;/strong&gt;. However, for specific protected areas or if you plan to engage in activities beyond standard trail running, it's always best to check with the local park authorities for any specific regulations or permit requirements.

Q: What do other runners say about jogging in Verín?
A: The running routes around Verín are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from tranquil river valleys to challenging mountain trails, and the opportunity to combine physical activity with exploring historical sites and natural beauty.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant elevation gain for a challenging workout?
A: Yes, the mountainous and hilly terrain around Verín provides excellent opportunities for gradient training. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2198022840/circular-ruta-to-minas-dos-carris-peneda-geres-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Ruta to Minas dos Carris — Peneda-Gerês National Park&lt;/a&gt;, with over 950 meters of elevation gain, offer a demanding workout. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990590095/alto-de-amoreira-via-the-mines-of-the-shadows-serra-do-xures" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Amoreira via the Mines of the Shadows – Serra do Xurés&lt;/a&gt; also features significant climbs, engaging more muscles and providing an intense experience.

Q: Can I access running trails near Verín using public transport?
A: While some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991629737/pozo-de-demo-circular-from-verin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de Demo - circular from Verín&lt;/a&gt;, start directly from the town, access to trails further afield, especially within natural parks, often requires personal transport. Verín has local bus services, but connections to specific trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local public transport schedules if you plan to rely on it for trail access.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by hot springs or natural pools?
A: Yes, the region is known for its thermal waters. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24549636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Caldo Hot Springs loop from Os Baños&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.9 km route that takes you near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Caldo Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find natural pools like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corga da Fecha Natural Pool&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into longer runs or visited as a post-run cool-down spot.
tips:
Magnificent waterfall in the Rego das Quintas, a tributary of the Abedes River that rises on Mount Fraga de Lobos.
Due to the large rocks that surround the well, it is impossible to see the full magnitude of this great waterfall.

A sign, next to the sound of water, indicates the descent to the river. Although the path is quite accessible, you have to be careful not to slip on rainy days.

To be able to see the waterfall, you have to look below, in an opening between the stones.

The Cabreiroá Spa is one of Galicia's most emblematic thermal resorts, renowned for the quality of its mineral-medicinal waters and a history that blends science, modernist architecture, and thermal tradition.
As early as the 19th century, a pool existed there, used by locals to treat stomach and bladder problems, and to promote wound healing.
